mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer and capital demand, boosting aggregate economic activity and generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52% over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production below zero during periods of high inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, causing immediate negative market repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat when INDPRO turns negative during high inflation periods, contradicting the margin compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and core inflation creates a positive real interest rate environment that stabilizes currency purchasing power and reduces inflation risk premiums, supporting positive equity valuations over one month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds CPIAUCSL annualized growth by > 0.5%
failure_condition: Positive real rate differentials consistently coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns, yielding a negative Rank IC over any 18-month sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the employed population and aggregate wage income, which directly fuels consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day forward returns, with a rolling 3-year Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real gross domestic product signals an economic recession that immediately triggers risk-off positioning and margin calls, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns within the following trading week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in over 60% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts, invalidating the recession risk-off mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable employment indicates efficient capacity utilization and healthy corporate output growth, which improves profit margins and generates positive equity returns during mid-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: The combination of rising INDPRO and stable PAYEMS fails to predict positive 20-day returns during mid-cycle phases, with directional accuracy below 50%.
